mnemonics: F.V.E.D. - Finite graphs have a finite number of Vertices (nodes) and Edges (links)
keyPoints: A graph consists of vertices (nodes) and edges (connections)
A finite graph has a finite number of both vertices and edges
Graph theory has applications in computer networks, social networks, and transportation systems
commonMistakes: Assuming all graphs are finite (some theoretical graphs are infinite)
Confusing vertices with edges when drawing or analyzing graphs
Forgetting that graphs can have multiple edges between the same vertices (multigraphs)
